questions.q: Akbar belonged to which Indian dynasty?
questions.explanation: Akbar was the third emperor of the Mughal dynasty founded by his grandfather Babur.

questions.q: Akbar is the third emperor of which empire?
questions.explanation: Akbar was the third ruler of the Mughal Empire, after Babur and Humayun.

questions.q: By what honorific title is Akbar most popularly known?
questions.explanation: He is commonly remembered as Akbar the Great for his wide-ranging reign and reforms.

questions.q: Who was Akbar's father?
questions.explanation: Akbar succeeded his father Humayun, who died in 1556 shortly after reclaiming Delhi.

questions.q: Who succeeded Akbar as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Akbar was succeeded in 1605 by his son Salim, who took the regnal name Jahangir.

questions.q: Akbar is especially remembered for which attitude toward different faiths?
questions.explanation: Akbar is celebrated for his policy of religious tolerance toward the diverse faiths of his empire.

questions.q: What was the name of the syncretic faith promoted by Akbar?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced Din-i-Ilahi, an experimental faith blending elements of several religions.

questions.q: In which century did most of Akbar's reign take place?
questions.explanation: Akbar reigned from 1556 to 1605, placing the bulk of his rule in the 16th century CE.

questions.q: Akbar came to the throne as a young boy at roughly what age?
questions.explanation: Akbar became emperor in 1556 at about thirteen years of age after Humayun's death.

questions.q: Akbar greatly did what to the Mughal Empire during his long reign?
questions.explanation: Akbar greatly expanded and consolidated the Mughal Empire through conquest and administrative reform.

questions.q: In what year did Akbar become Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Akbar ascended the throne in 1556 following his father Humayun's death.

questions.q: Who served as the regent and protector of the young Akbar in the early years of his reign?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan acted as regent and guided the boy-emperor Akbar, including at the Second Battle of Panipat.

questions.q: Akbar's land-revenue and administrative reforms were largely organised by which minister?
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal devised the dahsala revenue settlement system that underpinned Akbar's administration.

questions.q: What military and administrative ranking system did Akbar institute to organise his nobility?
questions.explanation: Akbar created the mansabdari system, ranking officials by numerical mansabs that fixed their status and obligations.

questions.q: Which capital city did Akbar build near Agra in the 1570s?
questions.explanation: Akbar founded the planned city of Fatehpur Sikri, which briefly served as his capital.

questions.q: The hall where Akbar held religious discussions among scholars of different faiths was called the?
questions.explanation: Akbar built the Ibadat Khana (House of Worship) at Fatehpur Sikri for interfaith debates.

questions.q: Which Rajput general, a Mughal commander, fought for Akbar at the Battle of Haldighati in 1576?
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh of Amber led Akbar's forces against Maharana Pratap at Haldighati.

questions.q: Akbar abolished which tax that had been levied on non-Muslims?
questions.explanation: As part of his conciliatory policy, Akbar abolished the jizya, the poll tax on non-Muslims.

questions.q: The official chronicle of Akbar's reign, the Akbarnama, was written by whom?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl, a leading member of Akbar's court, composed the Akbarnama and its statistical appendix the Ain-i-Akbari.

questions.q: Which fort city in Rajasthan did Akbar capture after a famous siege in 1568?
questions.explanation: Akbar besieged and captured the Sisodia stronghold of Chittorgarh in 1568.

questions.q: Which Rajput princess did Akbar marry as part of his policy of alliance with the Rajputs?
options: A princess of Marwar's Rathore line first
questions.explanation: Akbar married a princess of the house of Amber, sealing an alliance with that Rajput clan.

questions.q: Akbar's birth in 1542 took place at which town?
questions.explanation: Akbar was born in 1542 at Umarkot in present-day Sindh, while Humayun was a fugitive.

questions.q: What was Akbar's full given name at birth?
questions.explanation: Akbar's full name was Jalal-ud-din Muhammad Akbar; Zahir-ud-din Muhammad was the name of his grandfather Babur.

questions.q: In which year did Akbar formally proclaim the Din-i-Ilahi?
questions.explanation: Akbar is generally said to have proclaimed the Din-i-Ilahi around 1582.

questions.q: Through which 1562 measure did Akbar signal early conciliation, by ending the practice of doing what to war captives?
options: Enslaving prisoners of war and their families
questions.explanation: In 1562 Akbar banned the enslavement of war captives and their families, an early sign of his conciliatory policy.

questions.q: Akbar created an inner group of elite disciples bound to him as part of his sovereignty doctrine. What concept expressed this idea of the emperor as spiritual guide?
options: Sulh-i-kul / the emperor as supreme arbiter
questions.explanation: Akbar promoted sulh-i-kul (universal peace) and a 1579 mahzar declaration that made him the final arbiter in religious disputes.

questions.q: Which wealthy region did Akbar annex in the 1570s, securing its rich textile and maritime trade for the empire?
questions.explanation: Akbar conquered Gujarat in 1572-1573, securing its rich textile and maritime trade; the victory is commonly linked to the 'Fatehpur' (city of victory) name.

questions.q: The Ain-i-Akbari, a detailed gazetteer of Akbar's empire, forms the third volume of which larger work?
questions.explanation: The Ain-i-Akbari is the third part of Abul Fazl's Akbarnama, cataloguing administration, revenue, and statistics.

questions.q: Which northern kingdom did Akbar annex in 1586, bringing its valley firmly under Mughal control?
questions.explanation: Akbar annexed Kashmir in 1586, adding the valley to the Mughal domains.

questions.q: In which year did Akbar die, and where was he buried?
questions.explanation: Akbar died in 1605 and was buried in a grand tomb at Sikandra near Agra.
